name: RDF Triple
description: Structure documentation for an RDF triple — the fundamental statement unit in the Resource Description Framework, consisting of subject, predicate, and object.

fields.name: subject
fields.description: The resource being described, expressed as an IRI or blank node identifier.

fields.name: predicate
fields.description: The property or relationship connecting the subject to the object, always expressed as an IRI.

fields.name: object
fields.description: The value or resource the subject is related to via the predicate. Can be an IRI reference or an RDF literal with optional datatype and language tag.

subfields.name: value
subfields.description: The lexical form of the literal value.

subfields.name: datatype
subfields.description: IRI identifying the datatype of the literal. Defaults to xsd:string.

subfields.name: language
subfields.description: BCP47 language tag for language-tagged string literals.
